name: Bonnie Li
affiliation: Google DeepMind (Research Engineer), McGill University
problems_worked_on: World models and embodied AI (Genie 2, SIMA 2)
what_makes_them_spiky: Contributor to Gemini 2.5 and Gemini 3 (Google's flagship AI models)
Key contributor to Genie 2 (large-scale foundation world model) and SIMA 2 (generalist embodied agent)
Started fundamental RL research at Mila under Yoshua Bengio at age 17
Published at ICML 2024, NeurIPS DRLW 2020
Experience at NVIDIA and Google before DeepMind
5,394+ followers on X/Twitter
key_work: Gemini 2.5 and Gemini 3 (Google's frontier AI models)
Genie 2: A Large-Scale Foundation World Model (key contributor)
SIMA 2: Generalist Embodied Agent for Virtual Worlds
LMAct: In-Context Imitation Learning with Long Multimodal Demonstrations (ICML 2024)
Domain Adversarial Reinforcement Learning (NeurIPS DRLW 2020)
current_work: Research Engineer at Google DeepMind working on frontier models (Gemini), world models (Genie), and embodied AI agents (SIMA)
alpha_insight: Bonnie is at the center of DeepMind's most ambitious projects: Gemini (frontier LLMs), Genie 2 (world models that can generate entire interactive 3D environments), and SIMA 2 (generalist agents that operate in virtual worlds). The convergence of these three lines of research - language understanding, world simulation, and embodied agency - is likely DeepMind's path toward AGI. Starting RL research under Yoshua Bengio at 17 shows exceptional early trajectory.

name: Spruce Campbell
affiliation: MIT Physics, prev OpenAI, Decart
what_makes_them_spiky: Co-author of Oasis: A Universe in a Transformer (Decart, 2024) - first real-time AI world model
Worked at OpenAI
Research at MIT KellisLab (Manolis Kellis' Computational Biology Group)
Invited to 10 Downing Street
Bridges physics, computational biology, and AI
MIT Physics background applied to AI/world models
key_work: Oasis: A Universe in a Transformer (2024) - real-time open-world AI model that generates interactive gameplay frame-by-frame
Research at MIT KellisLab on computational biology
Work at OpenAI
current_work: MIT Physics student exploring AI, bioengineering, and healthcare. Thinking about applying AI to the American healthcare system. Research at KellisLab.
alpha_insight: Oasis demonstrated that transformers can generate entire interactive worlds in real-time (47ms per frame) - a fundamental proof that AI can simulate reality, not just describe it. Spruce's pivot from world models to computational biology and healthcare suggests he sees an opportunity to apply similar generative/simulation approaches to biological systems. The combination of physics training, world model expertise, and computational biology research at KellisLab is an unusual and powerful intersection for AI-driven drug discovery or healthcare.
